note: ROOT CAUSE FOUND & RESOLVED — SUPERSEDES the Jun-9 'read-only key' diagnosis and today's 'account entitlement' hypotheses: the 3-session order-write block was a TradeZero BREAKING SCHEMA CHANGE on POST /v1/api/accounts/{acct}/order (changed ~Jun 8 close -> Jun 9 open). Two fields became REQUIRED: clientOrderId (unique string, also the id used for DELETE cancel) and route ('SMART'); quantity/price were renamed orderQuantity/limitPrice. A body missing these dies in gateway schema validation BEFORE auth with the misleading 400 invalid-character-'<' (hence the no-auth probe returning the identical error — it never reached auth either). Keys were NEVER the problem; owner supplied the correct format and orders worked instantly. WORKING SCHEMA (validated live): {clientOrderId, side:Buy|Sell, openClose:Open|Close, symbol, orderQuantity, orderType:Limit|Market, route:'SMART', timeInForce:'Day', securityType:'Stock', limitPrice}. Notes: Sell/Open auto-converts to SellShort; paper echoes route:'PAPER'; cancel = DELETE /orders/{clientOrderId}; under SSR a short limit must rest at/above the ask (slow fill). DIAGNOSIS LESSON: a gateway parse error can be SCHEMA validation, not auth — when a previously-working endpoint 400s opaquely, re-check the vendor's CURRENT docs/schema FIRST before concluding permissions; the identical-error-without-auth probe does NOT prove an auth-layer failure when body validation precedes auth.

note: BY-PRINCIPLE LOWER LIMIT must be read from the TRADED share's OWN chart, never another's.
